topic: AI Coding Tool Adoption Statistics 2026: JetBrains Survey of 10K Developers

key_points: Primary source: JetBrains AI Pulse survey (January 2026, 10,000+ developers, 8 languages)
90% of developers regularly use at least one AI tool at work
74% adopted specialized AI coding tools (not just chatbots)
GitHub Copilot: 76% awareness, 29% work adoption — growth stalled
Claude Code: 57% awareness, 18% work adoption — 6x growth from April-June 2025
Cursor: 69% awareness, 18% work adoption — growth slowing
Claude Code US/Canada adoption reached 24% in January 2026
Claude Code highest loyalty: CSAT 91%, NPS 54
Google Antigravity (launched Nov 2025): 6% adoption
JetBrains AI Assistant: 9%, Junie: 5%
ChatGPT for coding: 28%, Gemini chatbot: 8%, Claude chatbot: 7%
Sonar survey of 1,100 developers
72% use AI coding tools daily
42% of code is AI-generated or AI-assisted
AI-generated code share projected to reach 65% by 2027
96% don't fully trust AI-generated code
48% don't always review AI code before committing
Tool usage: GitHub Copilot (75%), ChatGPT (74%), Claude (48%), Gemini/Duet AI (37%), Cursor (31%)
64% have used AI agents, 25% use them regularly
88% report encountering issues with AI code (unreliable 53%, duplicative 40%)
57% cite exposure of sensitive data concerns
Stack Overflow 2025: 84% use or plan to use AI tools (up from 76% in 2024)
47.1% of developers use AI tools daily
17.7% use AI tools weekly
32.7% trust AI output; 45.7% distrust AI output
76% say AI increases productivity; 70% spend extra time debugging AI code
Global software developer population: 28.7 million
66% of developers cite AI solutions that are 'almost right, but not quite'
Analysis of JetBrains 2026 survey data with awareness vs. usage gap breakdown
GitHub Copilot: 76% awareness vs 29% usage (47-point gap)
Cursor has highest conversion rate: 35.5% of aware developers become users
Gartner: 40% of enterprise apps will incorporate AI agents by end of 2026
82% of developers haven't yet integrated AI coding tools into their workflow (opportunity)
84% of developers now use AI coding tools daily
Only 29% trust the output in production
Key insight: gap between 'it works' and production readiness
Cursor and Claude Code highlighted as leading tools

stats.fact: 90% of developers regularly used at least one AI tool at work for coding and development tasks as of January 2026
stats.source: JetBrains AI Pulse Survey, January 2026 (10,000+ developers)

stats.fact: 74% of developers worldwide had adopted specialized AI tools for developers (coding assistants, editors, agents) by January 2026
GitHub Copilot has 76% developer awareness and 29% work adoption as of January 2026, though its growth has stalled
Claude Code work adoption grew 6x from ~3% in April-June 2025 to 18% in January 2026
Claude Code reached 24% adoption in the US and Canada by January 2026
Claude Code holds the highest product loyalty metrics: CSAT of 91% and NPS of 54
Cursor: 69% awareness and 18% work adoption as of January 2026; growth has slowed
GitHub Copilot is adopted by 40% of developers in companies with over 5,000 employees
Google Antigravity reached 6% adoption rate just two months after launching in November 2025
84% of developers use or plan to use AI tools, up from 76% in 2024
47.1% of developers use AI tools daily; only 16.2% have no plans to use AI tools
72% of developers who have tried AI use it nearly every day
stats.source: Sonar State of Code Survey 2026 (1,100 developers)

stats.fact: 42% of code being created today involves AI assistance; projected to reach 65% by 2027
96% of developers do not fully trust that AI-generated code is functionally correct
Only 32.7% of developers trust AI output; 45.7% actively distrust it
AI-generated code contains 2.74x more vulnerabilities than human-written code
stats.source: Modall AI in Software Development Report 2026

stats.fact: 62% of developers rely on at least one AI-powered coding assistant, agent, or code editor
stats.source: JetBrains Developer Ecosystem Survey 2025 (24,534 developers, 194 countries)

stats.fact: Nearly 9 in 10 developers save at least one hour per week using AI tools; 1 in 5 save 8+ hours per week
68% of developers expect employers to require proficiency in AI tools in the near future
76% of developers say AI increases productivity, but 70% also report spending extra time debugging AI-generated code
JetBrains AI Pulse survey sample: 10,000+ professional developers in 8 languages, globally representative with raking weighting
stats.source: JetBrains AI Pulse Survey methodology, January 2026

stats.fact: Gartner predicts 40% of enterprise applications will incorporate AI agents by the end of 2026

angles: The JetBrains AI Pulse survey (10K+ developers) provides the most credible picture of actual work adoption vs. mere awareness — a crucial distinction
The 'performance over platform' shift: Claude Code's 6x growth shows developers will abandon ecosystem lock-in for a clearly superior tool
The trust paradox: 90% use AI tools but only 29-33% trust the output — revealing the gap between adoption and reliance
Tool consolidation underway: The market is narrowing to three leaders (GitHub Copilot, Claude Code, Cursor) while early movers stagnate
Enterprise vs. individual adoption diverges: GitHub Copilot dominates at large enterprises (40% in 5,000+ employee firms) while Claude Code leads at startups and individual developers
The 8-language survey design and global sample sizes make JetBrains data more reliable than smaller or English-only surveys
The 6x growth of Claude Code in one year is the fastest adoption trajectory in professional developer tooling history
Practical guide angle: how developers should choose between the top three tools based on actual survey data rather than marketing
keywords: AI coding tool awareness vs usage

H2: The Trust Gap: High Adoption, Low Confidence in AI Output
H2: Enterprise vs. Individual: How Adoption Splits by Company Size
H2: How the AI Coding Tool Market Will Shift in 2026
